01 — Market Research
Who we're building for?
Most fintechs are built for beginners or billionaires. Our user is neither. They earn well, they think about money, and they've never had a platform that actually knows their situation. This isn't cold acquisition, it's 'conversion'.
🎯
The Stuck Saver
Primary ICP
Has 2–3 SIPs running but no clear plan. Money is moving but just not toward anything specific. No FIRE target, no advisor.
What 1% Club gives themBaskets: instead of researching 5 funds, one expert-built portfolio.
🧩
The Actively Confused
Secondary ICP
Has MFs, stocks, FDs scattered across Zerodha, Groww, and bank accounts. Too financially aware for generic advice, too busy to fix it.
What 1% Club gives themAI Assistant pulls actual data and tells them what to do next. Actual real insights, not generic algorithm advice.
🔍
The Trust Seeker
Tertiary ICP
Bank advisors sell what earns them commission while private wealth managers have ₹1Cr minimums. No middle ground exists.
What 1% Club gives themUnbiased Pillow insurance. Satellite Baskets for higher-conviction investing.
⚔️

02 — Distribution & Channel Strategy

One clear entry point. Everything else follows.

The app may contain Baskets, Mutual Funds, FDs, Insurance from day one, but that doesn't mean we market all of them together.

When everything is the hero, nothing is. Every few weeks, a specific product gets its own spotlight through dedicated content, creator partnerships, onboarding flows, and community activation.

The 9-Week Launch Plan

Build the problem. Build the anticipation. Launch. Expand.

Build the Problem
Jul 14 – Jul 20
Surface the pain
Sharan and Raghav appear on a podcast. 1% club opens up forms for beta users
Build Anticipation
Jul 21 – Aug 10
Beta users push
Beta users post about their real experience. Sharan reveals one feature.
Launch
Aug 11 – Aug 17
App goes live
Baskets + AI + Money School go live. Founder Pricing activates.
Expand
Aug 18 – Oct 19
New products
1% club launches FDs | Insurance | Credit card management
See full 9-week breakdown →
📊
Channel Prioritization Framework
P0 → P3 · what gets attention and when
▾
P0
Sharan's Content

Primary trust engine and largest acquisition source. Non-negotiable.

P1
Existing Members

6L+ community members who already trust the brand. The launch should feel like something they are part of, not something happening to them.

P2
Referral Program

Lowest CAC of any growth channel. Referred users convert faster and retain longer.

P3
SEO

A lot of our ideal customers are already on internet searching for - "when can I retire India" and it is a more qualified lead than someone who saw a reel.

Governing Principle1% Club should exhaust owned and earned distribution before scaling paid acquisition. The community is the moat.
Offline Activation

Campaigns that make people stop and stare.

Insight: Most people don't realize how dependent they are on their next paycheck. Offline ads creates a visual representation of being trapped in the cycle of earning without a clear path to financial freedom.

Offline · OOH · Print Media

03 — Marketing & Demand Generation

Brand Positioning

Brand Positioning
🛡
Trust
SEBI-registered, no hidden commissions.
📚
Education
3 years of content that already taught the audience how money works.
⚡
Action
Every insight connects to an execution — learn it and buy it in the same app.
Founder Pricing Strategy

Don't call it a discount. Call it 'Founder Pricing' — only the first 10,000 members. Make signing up on Aug 15 feel different from Sep 15.

Monthly
₹499
per month
RECOMMENDED
Annual
₹2,999
vs ₹3,999 · Save ₹1,000
This is the price you pay for believing in us before we were everywhere.
Founder Pricing 6,847 of 10,000 spots claimed
Influencer Strategy
Tier 1 — Credibility Anchors · 1–2 people
Respected voices whose endorsement signals 1% Club is serious. Nikhil Kamath posts "Here's why I backed this" on launch week — not sponsored, genuine.
Tier 2 — Reach Drivers · 5–8 creators
Early app access 2 weeks before launch. No brief, no script. Authentic reactions beat scripted posts every time.
Tier 3 — Community Amplifiers · 20–30 micro creators
Physical mailer: premium envelope, handwritten note from Sharan, early access. They post because they feel special — not because they were paid.
Affiliate Marketing
What counts as an action
FIRE calculator completion · Membership started · First Basket invested · First FD made
Stage 1 — Acquisition
User onboards on the app, and takes atleast one action.
Stage 2 — Retention
Same user returns and engages with a second product. This is owned by our internal CRM — not affiliate spend.
Stage 3 — Engagement
User is active across 2+ products, has referred at least one friend. Led by internal CRM
Performance Marketing
Activates Aug 15 · Amplifier, not engine

Paid media closes gaps that organic can't reach.

01
Warm, unconverted
Engaged with content. Never clicked through. They know us, they just haven't acted.
Retargeting
›
02
High-intent strangers
Actively searching FIRE, retirement, financial planning. Intent is there, we just need to appear.
Google Search
›
03
Cold, profile-matched
Don't know us. But they look exactly like people who already pay for membership.
Meta Lookalikes
Meta Ads
Post launch only
No spend before launch. Waitlist + referrals + Sharan's content handles pre-launch. Paid activates when there's something real to convert toward.
Objective
App installs + Membership conversions
Creative Direction
Social proof leads every ad.
No feature lists. No product explainers. Show real people who found their number and acted.
The creative job is not to explain what the app does, it's to make the audience feel like they're the last person who doesn't have their FIRE number.
🎯
The highest ROI pool — Retargeting
Users who completed the FIRE calculator but didn't buy membership. They've seen the value, know the product, and didn't convert. Every creative iteration goes here first.
Priority
#1
04 — Onboarding & Activation

From waitlist to first transaction.

Pre-Launch Waitlist

1

Day of Signup

WhatsApp from 1% Club: "You're in". Screen says "You're #847 in line" — not just "on the list".

2

One Week Before Launch

"Find your FIRE number before anyone else." Links to a web FIRE calculator — no app download needed.

3x more likely to convert on launch day
3

Launch Day

"It's live. You're early. Here's your link." Direct deep link — zero friction between message and first session.

Referral Program Rules

Who Can Refer
Members only — premium feature.
New User Gets
1 week free trial. One per number, no gaming it.
Referring Member Gets
2 weeks added to Membership. No cash. No codes.

ASO and SEO

ASO
Launch week will redirect a lot of users to Playstore and Appstore. Focus: Ratings · Reviews · Keywords
Custom Store Listings
Investment app India · mutual fund app · retirement calculator · financial planning app · wealth management app
SEO
Technical SEO: Sitemap, Page speed, Redirect issues, Broken link audit
On-Page SEO: Title and meta description
Off-Page SEO: Domain Authority, Backlinking
05 — Success Metrics

What success looks like at every stage.

Launch Week — 7-Day Signals

North Star
Membership Conversions
Volume Signal
App downloads + FIRE calculator completions
Quality Signal
Conversion rate: download → membership
Retention Signal
30-day retention by acquisition source
Community Signal
Referrals sent + FIRE numbers shared on social
ASO Signal
App Store rating 4.4+ within 30 days

Channel Performance & Budget Allocation
Tracking by source · Budget framework
▾
ChannelWhat We TrackHow We Track
YouTubeViews, watch time, CTR on description linkYouTube Studio + UTM links
InstagramReel plays, saves, shares, link-in-bio clicksInstagram Insights + Linktree UTMs
LinkedIn / XImpressions, link clicks, app store clicksNative analytics + UTM per post
OOHQR scans, city-wise breakdownUTM-tagged QR codes per installation
Zepto Scratch CardQR scans, installs, FIRE calculator completionsUTM-tagged QR code on card back
Referral ProgramReferrals sent, conversion rate, member qualityIn-app referral tracking by phone number
Google SearchKeyword rankings, organic installs, CTR by queryGoogle Search Console + Play Store analytics
Budget Allocation Framework

Owned trust channels first. Paid amplification second. As CAC data matures, performance marketing scales.
